items.headline: Illinois' Blue Envelope Program aims to help drivers with autism during traffic stops
items.summary: Starting Jan. 1, 2027, drivers can get a free blue envelope at their DMV to hold their vehicle documents. Similar programs have been established across the U.S.

items.headline: DMV services returning to Aledo, Illinois Secretary of State confirms
items.summary: Since the previous location's closure last year, Aledo residents have had to travel to DMV facilities in Silvis, Monmouth and Galesburg for in-person services.

items.headline: Semi truck driver airlifted to hospital after crash that landed in Linn County creek
items.summary: According to an Iowa State Patrol crash report, the semi truck suffered a tire failure while traveling north on Interstate 380.

items.headline: New Martin Luther King Jr. mural dedicated in Davenport

items.headline: Bettendorf expanding park cameras after vandalism causes thousands of dollars in damage
items.summary: Kiwanis Park currently has three security cameras, and city officials are now planning to install additional cameras at other parks.

items.headline: Illinois Tollway seeks public input on $26.5 billion plan

items.headline: Silvis Ethics Commission holds hearing following alderman's resignation
items.summary: Former Alderman Craig Pirmann was accused of recording a closed-door City Council meeting.
